sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 3 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 29, level 1.
Valid actions right now: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position - collects green blocks VERIFIED(step 2)', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns; can trigger board reconfiguration VERIFIED(step 14)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks, collectable by MOUSE', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple (left) + white (right) at row 63, likely progress/timer'}
- Cross-level notes: Green blocks respawn after board reconfiguration. Multiple phases possible within a level.
Current level:
- World model: Level 1, phase 2. Board reconfigured. 9 green blocks in 2 groups: 3 in a row (rows 13-17, cols 25-41) and 6 in 2x3 grid (rows 31-41, cols 13-29). Player at cols 50-52 in bottom sky blue region.
- Goal model: Collect all green blocks by clicking them, then move player to trigger next phase
- Recent findings: ['Board reconfigured after moving RIGHT 3 times (step 14)', '9 green blocks appeared in new positions', 'Player moved to cols 50-52', 'Sky blue now split into 2 regions: top (rows 1-29) and bottom (rows 37-62)', 'Purple bar expanded from 8 to 13 pixels']
- Open questions: ['How many phases total?', 'Does collecting all blocks in each phase advance the game?', 'What triggers board reconfiguration?']
- Plan: Collect all 9 green blocks by clicking their centers
- Strategy log: ['Moving player RIGHT triggered board change - may be intentional mechanic']
- Failed probes: ['ACTION7 - unknown action']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 28 actions; a strong score needs about 21 or fewer. You are 1.3x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Moving LEFT also didn't trigger a board change. The player just moved left within the sky blue region. The board looks the same - just the player position changed.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics:
1. Phase 1: 7 green blocks, collected them, moved RIGHT which triggered reconfiguration
2. Phase 2: 9 green blocks, collected them, but neither RIGHT nor LEFT triggered reconfiguration
Maybe the level is complete and I'm just not seeing it? Or maybe there's a different trigger.
Wait, let me look at the image more carefully. The sky blue region now appears to be a single connected region (pixels=2117, rows=1-62, cols=13-53). Previously in phase 2, there were two separate sky blue regions. This is interesting - the sky blue region seems to have merged or changed shape.
Actually, looking at the image, the sky blue region has a winding shape that goes from top to bottom. The player is in the middle-right area.
Let me think about what could complete the level:
1. Maybe I need to reach a specific corner or location
2. Maybe I need to use MOUSE on something other than green blocks
3. Maybe the level completes when the player reaches the top-left or bottom-right corner
Let me try clicking on the player itself, or try to navigate to a specific location.
Actually, let me look at the bottom bar more carefully. The purple bar might indicate progress. Let me check if it changed.
Wait, I should also consider that maybe the level is about navigating the player through the sky blue maze to reach a specific exit point. Let me try moving to the top-left corner of the sky blue region.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me check if the board has changed at all by comparing the current frame to the previous one. If nothing has changed except the player position, then I need to try something else.
Let me try clicking on the player position to see if that does anything.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and try clicking on it
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'Y') and n['pixels'] <= 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Player: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Try clicking on player
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 32}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Clicks on position - collects green blocks VERIFIED(step 2)", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns; triggered board reconfiguration once VERIFIED(step 14)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"background": "black with gray dots", "green_blocks": "light green 21-pixel blocks, collectable by MOUSE", "player": "blue+yellow small object", "platforms": "large sky blue regions"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "purple (left) + white (right) at row 63, likely progress/timer"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": "Green blocks respawn after board reconfiguration. Multiple phases possible within a level."}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1, phase 2 complete. All green blocks collected. Sky blue is single connected region (rows 1-62, cols 13-53). Player moved LEFT 3 times, no board change.", "goal_model": "Find what completes level - maybe need to reach specific location or click something", "recent_findings": ["Neither RIGHT nor LEFT triggered reconfiguration after collecting blocks", "Sky blue region is now one connected shape"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?", "Is there a specific target location?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking on player or navigate to specific location", "strategy_log": ["RIGHT after collecting blocks did nothing", "LEFT after collecting blocks did nothing"], "failed_probes": ["ACTION7", "RIGHT after collecting blocks", "LEFT after collecting blocks"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Player: rows=37-40, cols=33-34
Player: rows=38-39, cols=32-32
